description_long: This object consists of a group of five polygonal barrels, each with a faceted, low-poly design and a warm brown color. The barrels vary in size, with one noticeably larger and wider than the others, and all feature two horizontal, light gray bands encircling their bodies, suggesting structural reinforcement. The tops and bottoms are flat, with no visible openings or lids, and the barrels are arranged closely together, some partially overlapping. The surfaces appear matte and slightly rough, with no visible handles, spouts, or decorative markings. The barrels evoke a utilitarian style, suitable for storage or decorative use in urban or rustic settings. Their simple geometric form and lack of fine detail suggest they are intended for general-purpose use in environments such as alleys, courtyards, or storage areas.
description: A group of five brown, faceted barrels with light gray horizontal bands and flat tops, arranged closely together. The barrels are simple, matte, and lack handles or markings.
description_view_0: Front view showing all five barrels, with the largest barrel in the center and the others clustered around it.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the staggered arrangement and the overlapping placement of the barrels.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the largest barrel and two smaller barrels, with the tops of the barrels clearly visible.
description_view_3: Side view emphasizing the height of the largest barrel and the relative sizes of the surrounding barrels.
attribute_banding_or_reinforcement: light gray metal hoops at top and bottom
